skill.name: webflow-automation
skill.description: Automate Webflow CMS collections, site publishing, page management, asset uploads, and ecommerce orders via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Webflow CMS content, site publishing, pages, assets, and ecommerce orders through Composio's Webflow toolkit with Rube MCP integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 158 external command patterns and 1 network reference. All external command detections are FALSE POSITIVES - they identify MCP tool names in documentation (e.g., WEBFLOW_LIST_WEBFLOW_SITES), not actual shell execution. The network finding references the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint (rube.app/mcp) required for skill operation. MD5 usage is for file integrity verification during S3 uploads, not cryptographic security. No malicious intent detected. Skill is safe for publication with minor documentation warnings about production actions.

low_findings.title: Production Site Publishing Capability
low_findings.description: The skill can publish Webflow sites to production, making all staged changes live. This could cause unintended production deployments if misused.

low_findings.title: CMS Item Deletion Capability
low_findings.description: The skill supports permanent deletion of CMS collection items via DELETE_COLLECTION_ITEM. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone.

content.user_title: Automate Webflow CMS and Site Operations
content.value_statement: Managing Webflow content manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates CMS operations, site publishing, and asset management through Rube MCP integration with Composio's Webflow toolkit.
actual_capabilities: List, create, update, and delete Webflow CMS collection items with proper schema validation
Publish Webflow sites to production with custom domain or subdomain targeting
Upload assets (images, files) with base64 encoding and MD5 integrity verification
List and inspect site pages including DOM structure retrieval
Retrieve ecommerce order data with status filtering
Navigate Webflow's 24-character hexadecimal ID system for sites, collections, items, and pages
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Webflow OAuth authentication
Different OAuth scopes required for different operations (sites:read, cms:write, etc.)
Site publishing is rate-limited to 1 publish per minute
File uploads require base64-encoded content and manually computed MD5 hash

use_cases.title: Content Manager Bulk CMS Operations
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can批量 create blog posts, update product listings, or manage team member directories by calling CMS tools in sequence with proper field slug resolution.
use_cases.target_user: Content managers and marketing operations teams

use_cases.title: Developer Site Deployment Workflow
use_cases.description: Developers can automate site publishing after staged changes, verify page structures via DOM inspection, and manage multiple site environments programmatically.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers managing client Webflow sites

use_cases.title: Ecommerce Order Monitoring
use_cases.description: Store operators can retrieve order data, filter by status, and integrate order information into external reporting or fulfillment workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: List Available Webflow Sites
prompt_templates.prompt: List all Webflow sites I have access to and show me their IDs and names.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Webflow automation to discover available sites

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: View CMS Collections
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all CMS collections for the site with ID [site_id] and explain what each collection contains.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring existing CMS structure before making changes

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Create Blog Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new blog post in the blog-posts collection with title "[Title]", slug "[post-slug]", and content "[content]". First verify the collection schema to get correct field slugs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding new CMS content with proper schema validation

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Complete CMS Workflow with Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add 3 new team members to our team collection. For each person, create a CMS item with their name, role, bio, and headshot. Then publish the site to make changes live. Confirm with me before publishing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-step CMS operations culminating in production deployment

output_examples.output: Returns array of site objects with id, name, shortName, and customDomains. Example: [{id: "580e63fc8c9a982ac9b8b745", name: "My Site", shortName: "mysite", customDomains: ["example.com"]}]
Returns created item with _id, slug, created timestamp, and field data. Confirms item is staged and requires publishing to go live.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before any Webflow operations
Call GET_COLLECTION to retrieve field slugs before creating or updating CMS items - never guess field names
Confirm with users before executing PUBLISH_SITE or DELETE_COLLECTION_ITEM as these are irreversible production actions
anti_patterns: Using field display names instead of slugs in CMS operations - always retrieve schema first
Publishing without reviewing staged changes - PUBLISH_SITE deploys ALL pending changes
Assuming all sites have ecommerce enabled - verify before calling order-related endpoints

faq.question: How do I connect Webflow to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit "webflow". If not active, follow the auth link to complete OAuth.

faq.question: Why are my CMS changes not appearing on my live site?
faq.answer: CMS operations stage changes but don't publish them. Call PUBLISH_SITE with appropriate domain targets to deploy changes to production.

faq.question: What is the difference between field slugs and display names?
faq.answer: Display names are human-readable labels shown in Webflow UI. Slugs are URL-safe identifiers used in API calls. Always use slugs (e.g., "author-name") in field_data, not display names (e.g., "Author Name").

faq.question: Why do I get 403 errors when creating CMS items?
faq.answer: Your OAuth connection may lack required scopes. CMS write operations need cms:write scope. Re-authenticate with expanded permissions if needed.

faq.question: Can I upload images directly from URLs?
faq.answer: No. The UPLOAD_ASSET endpoint requires base64-encoded file_content and manually computed MD5 hash. You must first download and encode the image data.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large collections?
faq.answer: Use offset and limit parameters on LIST_COLLECTION_ITEMS and LIST_PAGES. Increment offset by limit until returned results are fewer than the limit.
